_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: Two events A and B satisfy the equality P(A and B) = P(A) · P(B). What condition does that equality express about the probability of event B when event A occurs?
options: That the two events are independent, so that the probability of event B does not change when event A occurs
author_rationale: The equality P(A ∩ B) = P(A)·P(B) is precisely the definition of independence. The author did not notice that the correct option is far longer than the rest and that it reuses the stem's wording about events A and B and the probability of B when A occurs.

intended_defect.description: Double deterministic leak: the correct option is more than twice the mean option length and repeats the stem's vocabulary ('two events', 'probability', 'event B', 'when event A occurs'). On top of that, 'That they add up to 1' and 'That they are equal' are vague distractors that do not correspond to any identifiable conceptual error.
intended_defect.expected_finding: item_probe must raise answer_length_flag (ratio above LENGTH_HIGH) and lexical_overlap_flag (stem↔answer overlap above OVERLAP_HIGH). The distractor reviewer must point out that only 'mutually exclusive' captures a real conceptual error (confusing independence with mutual exclusivity); the other two are filler.
